title: Physician Scheduling Software | ACGME-Compliant | Scheduling Wizard
description: Physician scheduling software built for GME programs. We deliver finished, ACGME-compliant schedules so chief residents can focus on medicine, not software.
paragraph: Stop wrestling with complex physician scheduling software. Simply send us your constraints, and our Scheduling Wizard service delivers a complete, ACGME-compliant schedule.

cta.title: Stop Searching for Software. Get Schedules Done.
cta.description: Join 18 departments across 13 hospitals who already outsource physician scheduling to experts.

faq.answer: Physician scheduling software typically refers to self-service tools that require your team to learn, configure, and operate a platform. Scheduling Wizard takes a different approach entirely: it is a managed service where your program submits its constraints and receives finished, optimized schedules delivered as Excel spreadsheets. There is no software for your team to learn or maintain. This done-for-you model eliminates the operator burden from chief residents and coordinators entirely.
faq.question: What is physician scheduling software and how does Scheduling Wizard differ?

faq.answer: ACGME duty hour compliance is mathematically enforced at the point of schedule generation, not manually reviewed afterward. The engine applies the 80-hour weekly limit, minimum rest requirements, the 24-hour hard cap, and subspecialty-specific ACGME rules relevant to your program. Because compliance is built into the optimization engine itself, violations are structurally prevented before the schedule is ever delivered to your program.
faq.question: How does Scheduling Wizard ensure ACGME compliance in physician schedules?

faq.answer: Scheduling Wizard delivers four types of schedules: Block and rotation schedules, Call schedules with fairness optimization, Clinic schedules, and Attending physician schedules. All schedule types are optimized simultaneously using a proprietary constraint-solving engine, so changes in one schedule automatically propagate across the others without manual cross-referencing.
faq.question: What types of physician schedules does Scheduling Wizard produce?

faq.answer: Yes. Many Scheduling Wizard clients continue using Amion or QGenda for day-to-day schedule viewing and display. Scheduling Wizard handles the schedule creation and optimization, delivering finished schedules as Excel files that your team can upload directly into your existing viewing platform. The two workflows are complementary, not mutually exclusive.
faq.question: Can Scheduling Wizard work alongside our existing scheduling tools like Amion or QGenda?

faq.answer: Subspecialty-specific ACGME rules are built directly into Scheduling Wizard's optimization engine. Rather than applying a generic compliance checklist, the engine applies the specific duty hour rules, rotation requirements, and call restrictions relevant to your program's specialty. ACGME compliance is the most common question on sales calls, and it is designed to be answered with confidence for each specific subspecialty.
faq.question: How does Scheduling Wizard handle subspecialty-specific ACGME scheduling rules?

faq.answer: Scheduling Wizard handles mid-year revisions as part of the managed service. When changes arise due to sick leave, parental leave, schedule swaps, or new constraints, the affected portions of the schedule are re-optimized without disrupting the entire published schedule. Programs submit the updated constraints and receive a revised schedule back, without needing to manually patch a spreadsheet or re-enter data into a software platform.
faq.question: What happens when a physician schedule needs to change mid-year?

hero.title: Physician Scheduling Software Delivered To You
hero.description: Stop wrestling with complex physician scheduling software. Submit your constraints and receive finished, ACGME-compliant schedules as ready-to-use Excel files.

items.title: Done For You Schedule Delivery
items.description: Unlike self-service physician scheduling software, Scheduling Wizard handles the entire scheduling process. Submit your constraints and receive finished Excel schedules — no software to operate, no learning curve, no annual retraining.

items.title: Compliance Built Into Every Schedule
items.description: ACGME duty hour regulations — including 80-hour weekly limits, rest requirements, and subspecialty-specific rules — are mathematically enforced at generation time. Violations are prevented before the schedule is ever published.

items.title: Year-Long Rotation Schedules Optimized
items.description: Generates complete year-long block rotation schedules respecting PGY-level requirements, elective preferences, coverage minimums, and ACGME rotation mandates across even the most complex multi-track programs.

items.title: Fair Call Schedules Every Time
items.description: On-call schedules are algorithmically optimized to distribute call, weekend, and holiday burden equitably across residents while enforcing duty hour limits and post-call rules — eliminating fairness disputes.

items.title: Scheduling Knowledge That Persists
highlights: New chiefs hit the ground running
items.description: Your program's scheduling rules, constraints, and configurations stay with Scheduling Wizard year over year. New chief residents inherit a fully working, optimized system instead of rebuilding from a blank spreadsheet.

features.title: Built for Physician and Residency Scheduling
features.description: Every capability is designed to eliminate scheduling burden from your program and deliver compliant, optimized schedules.

items.title: Software You Have to Master
items.description: Self-service physician scheduling software requires hours of setup, training, and rework — time chief residents and coordinators don't have.

items.title: Compliance Gaps Put Programs at Risk
items.description: Manually checking ACGME duty hour rules across block, call, and clinic schedules leaves room for costly violations that threaten accreditation.

items.title: Knowledge Lost Every Year
items.description: When the chief resident rotates out, all scheduling expertise walks out with them — leaving the next chief to start from scratch.

problems.title: Scheduling Software Isn't the Answer
problems.description: Most physician scheduling tools shift the burden onto you instead of solving the problem.

solution.title: Your Schedules Built and Delivered by Experts
solution.description: Scheduling Wizard is a done-for-you managed service, not another software tool to learn. Submit your program's constraints and receive finished, ACGME-compliant Block, Call, Clinic, and Attending schedules as ready-to-use Excel files. No platform to navigate. No retraining when chiefs rotate.

data.metaTitle: Physician Scheduling Software | ACGME-Compliant | Scheduling Wizard
data.metaDescription: Physician scheduling software built for GME programs. We deliver finished, ACGME-compliant schedules so chief residents can focus on medicine, not software.

searchIntents: As a chief resident, I need a scheduling solution that ensures our residency schedules comply with ACGME regulations without me having to become an expert in scheduling software.

I'm a program director looking for a replacement for our current scheduling method because it's too complicated and doesn't handle our specific subspecialty needs, leading to conflicts and gaps in coverage.
As a program coordinator, I want a way to automate the schedule generation process so I can save time and avoid constant back-and-forth with my team over scheduling disputes and compliance issues.

I manage a private practice and need a robust scheduling solution that not only handles physician availability but also integrates with my existing tools, unlike what's currently available in generic scheduling software.
